name: Against Malaria Foundation
mission: We help protect people from malaria We fund long-lasting insecticidal nets LLINs and work with distribution partners to ensure they are used. We track and report on net use and impact.
theoryOfChange: By providing 100% of public donations toward LLINs and ensuring their distribution and use through rigorous monitoring and data tracking, AMF reduces malaria illness and prevents deaths cost-effectively.
scoreSummary: Against Malaria Foundation (US) shows moderate impact and moderate alignment.

evaluationSources: The Life You Can Save - Top Rated 2012 to 2025
Giving What We Can - Top Rated 2012 to 2025

dimension_explanations.alignment: Alignment is rated lower as the organization maintains a secular, global health focus rather than a specific religious or cultural orientation [3]. While the cause is urgent and the track record is strong, it may not meet the specific 'donor fit' criteria for those seeking faith-based charitable pathways [6].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates moderate impact through its capacity-building model, focusing on large-scale net distributions that have shown measurable reductions in hospital admissions and malaria cases [4]. Its financial health is notable, with a Charity Navigator score of 94.0/100 and a commitment to directing all public donations toward program costs [1][6].

In the most recent fiscal period, the organization reported a total revenue of $119,395,632 while maintaining a 100.0% program expense ratio [1][2].

alignment.improvement: Alignment could be improved by <cite id="6">clearly stating the zakat-eligibility status</cite> of its programs to better serve Muslim donor requirements.

credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility further, the charity could <cite id="1">expand its board size</cite> beyond the current 4 members to enhance independent oversight.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize maximum financial efficiency and evidence-based impact, specifically those looking to provide life-saving sadaqah through a proven health intervention.
donor_motivations: Want every cent of their donation to go directly to the cause

ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fulfill zakat obligations or those who prefer supporting multi-sectoral community development projects.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade A due to <cite id="1">verified outcomes</cite>, <cite id="3">documented theory of change</cite>, and <cite id="1">100/100 Impact score</cite>.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: AMF provides LLINs to high-risk populations, monitors their use through surveys, and tracks malaria case data to reduce morbidity and mortality.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Rigorous evidence ensures that net distributions actually result in fewer malaria cases and deaths.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Malaria remains a leading cause of death in target regions, and AMF has the capacity to scale distributions as funding allows.

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="1">100.0% program expense ratio</cite> and a <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>, ensuring maximum impact for every donation.
AMF tracks specific outcomes, such as <cite id="4">hospital admissions due to malaria</cite>, and conducts <cite id="3">post-distribution net use surveys</cite> to verify effective usage.
The charity holds a <cite id="1">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite> and a <cite id="1">perfect 100/100 score for Culture & Accountability</cite> from Charity Navigator.

strengths_deep_dive: The Against Malaria Foundation's primary strength lies in its singular focus and lean operational model. By specializing exclusively in <cite id="3">long-lasting insecticidal nets</cite>, they achieve economies of scale and high levels of expertise in distribution logistics. Their commitment to transparency is evidenced by their <cite id="1">100/100 Impact & Measurement score</cite>, which reflects their practice of publishing detailed distribution reports and <cite id="3">malaria case data tracking</cite>. Furthermore, their financial model is unique in the nonprofit sector, as they ensure <cite id="6">100% of public donations</cite> are spent on nets, providing a clear and direct value proposition for donors seeking high-impact sadaqah.
rich_narrative.summary: The Against Malaria Foundation (US) is a highly specialized humanitarian organization dedicated to the <cite id="3">funding and distribution of long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs)</cite> to protect populations at high risk of malaria. Since its <cite id="1">founding in 2004</cite>, the organization has established a reputation for extreme financial efficiency, maintaining a <cite id="1">100.0% program expense ratio</cite>. This means that every dollar donated by the public is used exclusively to purchase nets, with administrative and fundraising costs covered by a small group of private donors. In the 2024 fiscal year, the organization reported <cite id="2">total revenue of $62,286,888</cite>, all of which was derived from contributions. AMF operates primarily in <cite id="3">Africa, Asia, and South America</cite>, with specific large-scale distributions in countries such as <cite id="3">Guinea, the Democratic Republic of Congo, and Nigeria</cite>. The organization's model is built on rigorous monitoring; they do not simply deliver nets but conduct <cite id="3">post-distribution surveys</cite> to ensure nets are being used correctly and remain in good condition. This data-driven approach has earned them an <cite id="1">overall score of 94.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite> and a <cite id="1">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite>.
